OBJ-1.evidence: BA added M10 (lines 78-82) requiring registration in policies/tool-policy.v1.json with narrow write paths and addition to CP_AGENTS in hooks/pretool-cp-checkin.py:60-72. AC11 (lines 333-344) tests policy_registry.is_allowed() with specific positive and negative test cases. The critical enforcement mechanism (policy_registry.py deny-all for unregistered roles) is now correctly scoped.

OBJ-2.evidence: M1 now reads git-read-only analyst subagent (filesystem writes restricted to /tmp/agentic-commit/push-analyst/ and optional ~/.claude/logs/ audit entry). M2 similarly corrected. The git-read-only phrasing precisely captures the intent.

OBJ-3.evidence: AC1 now explicitly requires push-analyst to read refs/remotes/<resolved_remote>/<branch>..HEAD and explicitly forbids @{u}..HEAD. Technical Hints line 364 references push.sh:38-42 fork-prefer-origin logic.

OBJ-4.evidence: BA added pull_exit_phase enum (success/rebase_conflict/stash_restoration_failed) and AC7 now covers the stash_restoration_failed case. However, the phase-inference logic is incorrect - see new BLOCKER OBJ-NEW-1.
OBJ-4.residual: The classification heuristic exit non-0 + HEAD changed = stash_restoration_failed fails for genuine rebase conflicts where git rebase partially applies commits before encountering a conflict, resulting in HEAD change.

OBJ-5.evidence: Context JSON observations (line 94) now states commands/pull.md lines 1-45 (wc -l confirmed 45 lines). However, context JSON component_chain entry at line 346-347 still says line_range: 1-46 - residual cosmetic inconsistency. Minor, does not block.

objections.title: Pull exit phase inference incorrectly classifies rebase-conflict-with-HEAD-change as stash_restoration_failed
objections.description: BA pull_exit_phase logic assumes: pull.sh exits non-0 AND pre_pull_head != post_pull_head => stash_restoration_failed. This is INCORRECT. When git pull --rebase encounters a merge conflict, git rebase partially applies commits before stopping, which CAN change HEAD (HEAD advances to the last successfully replayed commit). In this scenario: pull.sh exits 1 (rebase conflict path at line 151), HEAD has changed from pre-pull value, AND .git/rebase-merge or .git/rebase-apply directories exist. BA heuristic classifies this as stash_restoration_failed even though it is a rebase_conflict. Result: (1) AC7 stash_restoration_failed branch fires when it should not, showing misleading warning; (2) pull-analyst dispatched for mid-rebase-conflict with non-meaningful commit range. Codex verified empirically: rc=1, head_changed=yes, rebase_merge_exists=yes for genuine mid-rebase-conflict scenario.
evidence: hooks/pull.sh:120: git pull --rebase origin "$BRANCH"
hooks/pull.sh:151: exit 1 on rebase conflict (before stash pop is reached)
hooks/pull.sh:154: success message printed BEFORE stash pop
hooks/pull.sh:158-174: stash pop only reached after successful pull completion
hooks/pull.sh:174: exit 1 on stash-pop failure (genuinely different scenario from line 151)
BA spec AC7: uses pre_pull_head != post_pull_head as discriminant for stash_restoration_failed
Codex empirical confirmation: rc=1, head_changed=yes, rebase_merge_exists=yes during genuine rebase conflict
objections.required_fix: Replace HEAD-change heuristic with rebase-state-directory inspection: Derive pull_exit_phase by first checking rebase state: if pull.sh exits non-0 AND (.git/rebase-merge or .git/rebase-apply directory exists): set pull_exit_phase=rebase_conflict (HEAD may or may not have changed). If pull.sh exits non-0 AND no rebase state directory exists AND pre_pull_head != post_pull_head: set pull_exit_phase=stash_restoration_failed. If pull.sh exits non-0 AND no rebase state directory exists AND pre_pull_head == post_pull_head: unknown failure, treat as rebase_conflict for safety. Update AC7 to specify the rebase-state-directory check as the primary discriminant, not HEAD equality.

objections.title: Technical Hints contradict M10/AC11: claim all three roles have same allowed_tools including Write
objections.description: Technical Hints line 359 states: same allowed_tools list: [Read, Glob, Grep, Bash, Write] for all three analyst roles. However, M10 and AC11 explicitly require that pull-analyst excludes Write. Context JSON policy_registration section confirms pull-analyst gets allowed_tools: [Read, Glob, Grep, Bash] (no Write). This internal contradiction makes the spec ambiguous at the implementation level.
evidence: docs/dev/ticket-20260517-215114.md Technical Hints line 359: same allowed_tools list: [Read, Glob, Grep, Bash, Write]
docs/dev/ticket-20260517-215114.md M10 (lines 78-82): pull-analyst write restriction is narrower
docs/dev/ticket-20260517-215114.md AC11 (lines 333-344): negative test case for pull-analyst Write tool
docs/dev/context-20260517-215114.json policy_registration section: pull-analyst allowed_tools is [Read, Glob, Grep, Bash] without Write
objections.required_fix: Correct Technical Hints line 359 to distinguish roles: push-analyst and merge-analyst: allowed_tools: [Read, Glob, Grep, Bash, Write]; pull-analyst: allowed_tools: [Read, Glob, Grep, Bash] (no Write -- pull-analyst produces only console output, no grant token file).

objections.title: Optional audit log path (~/.claude/logs/) is not covered by M10/AC11 allowed_write_path_prefixes
objections.description: M1, M2, and S3 mention that analyst agents may write an optional audit log entry to ~/.claude/logs/. However, M10 and AC11 define the allowed_write_path_prefixes for push-analyst and merge-analyst as: [*/dev/null, */dev/stderr, */dev/stdout, */tmp/claude-scratch/push-analyst/, /tmp/agentic-commit/push-analyst/*]. The ~/.claude/logs/ path is absent from this list. A push-analyst or merge-analyst agent attempting the optional audit log write would be denied by policy_registry.py. This makes the optional audit log functionally impossible under the M10 policy, creating an internal contradiction between M1/M2/S3 (which permit it) and M10/AC11 (which forbid it by omission).
evidence: docs/dev/ticket-20260517-215114.md M1: optional ~/.claude/logs/ audit entry
docs/dev/ticket-20260517-215114.md M2: same optional audit entry
docs/dev/ticket-20260517-215114.md S3: asks for audit logging capability
docs/dev/context-20260517-215114.json policy_registration: push-analyst allowed_write_path_prefixes does not include ~/.claude/logs/ or equivalent home-directory paths
hooks/lib/policy_registry.py: write path check uses glob matching against allowed_write_path_prefixes; paths not matching are denied
objections.required_fix: Either: (a) Remove the optional audit log references from M1, M2, and S3 entirely -- the grant token itself is the audit record (recommended, avoids expanding write surface); OR (b) Add ~/.claude/logs/push-analyst-*.log and ~/.claude/logs/merge-analyst-*.log to the allowed_write_path_prefixes in M10 and AC11.

objections.title: Technical Hints still contains stale boolean stash_restoration_failure: true instead of pull_exit_phase enum value
objections.description: Technical Hints line 365 still reads stash_restoration_failure: true -- the old boolean field from the pre-revision design. The revised spec body correctly uses the enum: pull_exit_phase=stash_restoration_failed. A dev reading the Technical Hints to understand the context structure would implement the old boolean, while the normative AC7 requires the enum. The two are not backward-compatible.
evidence: docs/dev/ticket-20260517-215114.md Technical Hints line 365: stash_restoration_failure: true
docs/dev/ticket-20260517-215114.md AC7: uses pull_exit_phase enum
docs/dev/context-20260517-215114.json stash_pop_failure_rationale: correctly references enum
The boolean and enum are mutually exclusive data shapes -- a context JSON built from the Technical Hints hint will fail AC7 pull_exit_phase check
objections.required_fix: Replace Technical Hints line 365 'stash_restoration_failure: true' with 'pull_exit_phase: "stash_restoration_failed"' to align with the normative AC7 enum.

evidence_quality_detail: BA verified all affected source files with read confirmation and line number citations. The policy enforcement mechanism is now correctly identified (policy_registry.py + tool-policy.v1.json, not settings.json). Fork-prefer-origin logic at push.sh:38-42 is confirmed. Component chain entries include evidence_type and verified_via fields. The revised spec evidence quality is acceptable.
scope_alignment_detail: User requirement in Chinese: give me a plan. BA correctly scoped R2 as a design-plan output cycle. All three commands (push/merge/pull) are addressed. MoSCoW requirements cover three new agent files (M1-M3), three command body updates (M4-M6), grant token properties (M7-M8), DO NOT sections (M9), and new policy registration (M10). Scope alignment is preserved from iteration 1.
affected_file_accuracy_detail: All files BA identifies exist and contain the code BA claims. Minor residual: context JSON component_chain entry for commands/pull.md still says line_range: 1-46 while wc -l confirmed 45 lines. Does not block the spec.
spec_text_vs_execution_drift_detail: Two internal contradictions make the spec unexecutable as written: (1) Technical Hints line 359 says all three roles share the same allowed_tools including Write, while M10/AC11 require pull-analyst to exclude Write; (2) Technical Hints line 365 uses old boolean stash_restoration_failure: true while AC7 requires the pull_exit_phase enum. These contradictions would cause an implementation following the Technical Hints to fail AC11 and AC7 verification.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex identified 4 issues: (1) BLOCKER: Pull phase inference is incorrect -- git pull --rebase conflicts can change HEAD while leaving rebase-state directories, meaning 'exit non-0 + HEAD changed' does not reliably discriminate stash_restoration_failed from rebase_conflict; the correct discriminant is presence of .git/rebase-merge or .git/rebase-apply; (2) MAJOR: Technical Hints contradict M10/AC11 by claiming all three roles share the same allowed_tools including Write, while pull-analyst must exclude Write; (3) MAJOR: Optional ~/.claude/logs/ audit writes mentioned in M1/M2/S3 are not covered by M10/AC11 allowed_write_path_prefixes, making audit logging impossible under the specified policy; (4) MAJOR: Technical Hints still contains stale stash_restoration_failure: true boolean while spec body requires pull_exit_phase enum.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 4 Codex findings incorporated as new objections OBJ-NEW-1 through OBJ-NEW-4. BLOCKER finding changes draft verdict from PASS to FAIL. Three MAJOR internal contradictions also incorporated. The rebase-state-directory discriminant (OBJ-NEW-1) requires BA to update AC7 phase-inference logic.

summary: BA spec iteration 2 FAIL: all 5 prior objections resolved, but Codex adversarial review identified one BLOCKER (pull_exit_phase incorrectly classifies rebase-conflict-with-HEAD-change as stash_restoration_failed -- rebase state directories .git/rebase-merge/.git/rebase-apply must be checked as primary discriminant, not HEAD equality) and three MAJOR internal contradictions (Technical Hints claims all roles share allowed_tools including Write contradicting AC11 pull-analyst Write exclusion; ~/.claude/logs/ audit path mentioned in M1/M2/S3 is absent from M10/AC11 write policy; Technical Hints still uses stale stash_restoration_failure: true boolean instead of pull_exit_phase enum required by AC7).
